```{r interpretation-pubbias, echo=FALSE}
        "% probability that β > 0 means we cannot rule out the opposite pattern either.\n\n", sep = "")
  } else if (pr_neg_interp > 0.60) {
    cat("This is a weak tendency — only slightly above the 50% that would be expected by chance — ",
        "and provides no meaningful evidence of publication bias.\n\n")
  } else {
    cat("This is essentially a coin-flip between the two directions, providing no evidence of ",
        "systematic small-study bias.\n\n")
  }
} else if (beta_ci[2] < 0) {
  cat("The 95% CrI lies entirely below zero, providing evidence that smaller studies report systematically ",
      "larger protective effects for EEG-guided anaesthesia. This is consistent with publication bias, ",
      "though other explanations (e.g., differences in patient populations between smaller and larger trials) ",
      "cannot be excluded.\n\n")
} else {
  cat("The 95% CrI lies entirely above zero, suggesting smaller studies report weaker effects — ",
      "the opposite of the classic publication bias pattern. This provides reassurance against ",
      "selective publication of positive results.\n\n")
}

cat("Importantly, the **bias-adjusted models** provide a direct check on whether publication bias distorts the pooled estimate. ",
    "Both the selection-weight model (which down-weights studies based on their p-values) and the PET-PEESE model ",
    "(which extrapolates the effect to infinite sample size) produced pooled ORs consistent with the primary unadjusted estimate of ",
    sprintf("%.2f", or_median), ". If publication bias were substantially inflating the apparent benefit of EEG-guided anaesthesia, ",
    "these bias-adjusted models would have shifted the pooled OR meaningfully toward 1.0 — they did not.\n\n", sep = "")

cat("**Bottom line**: There is no strong evidence that publication bias is driving the results of this meta-analysis. ",
    "A mild tendency for smaller studies to report more favourable results (", sprintf("%.0f%%", 100 * pr_neg_interp),
    "% posterior probability) cannot be excluded, but the bias-adjusted estimates are reassuringly consistent with the primary analysis.\n", sep = "")
```

## Sequential Analysis

```{r interpretation-bsa, echo=FALSE}
#| label: interpretation-bsa
#| output: asis

# Final BSA results for all trials
bsa_final <- bsa_results[nrow(bsa_results), ]
bsa_crossed <- bsa_final$or_upper < 1

# Final BSA results for sensitivity
bsa_sens_final <- bsa_sensitivity[nrow(bsa_sensitivity), ]
bsa_sens_crossed <- bsa_sens_final$or_upper < 1

# Max assurance values
max_assurance_all <- max(ssd_all$pr_decisive, na.rm = TRUE)
max_assurance_filt <- max(ssd_filtered$pr_decisive, na.rm = TRUE)
best_n_all <- ssd_all$total_n[which.max(ssd_all$pr_decisive)]

cat("### All Trials\n\n")
cat("After accumulating all ", bsa_final$k, " studies (cumulative N = ",
    format(bsa_final$n_cumulative, big.mark = ","),
    "), the pooled OR is **", sprintf("%.2f", bsa_final$or_median),
    "** (95% CrI: ", sprintf("%.2f", bsa_final$or_lower), " to ",
    sprintf("%.2f", bsa_final$or_upper), "), with Pr(OR < 1) = ",
    sprintf("%.1f%%", 100 * bsa_final$pr_benefit), ".\n\n", sep = "")

if (bsa_crossed) {
  cat("The 95% CrI excludes 1.0 — the **efficacy boundary has been crossed**. ",
      "The cumulative evidence is sufficient to conclude that EEG-guided anaesthesia reduces ",
      "postoperative delirium when all study populations are included.\n\n")
} else {
  cat("The upper bound of the 95% CrI is ", sprintf("%.2f", bsa_final$or_upper),
      ", which remains above 1.0. This means the **efficacy boundary has not been crossed** — ",
      "despite a clear trend favouring EEG-guided anaesthesia (Pr(OR < 1) = ",
      sprintf("%.1f%%", 100 * bsa_final$pr_benefit),
      "), the existing evidence from ", bsa_final$k,
      " RCTs and ", format(bsa_final$n_cumulative, big.mark = ","),
      " patients is **not sufficient** to conclude with 95% certainty that the intervention reduces delirium.\n\n", sep = "")
}

cat("### Excluding Cardiac Surgery and Chinese Studies\n\n")
cat("When restricted to the ", bsa_sens_final$k,
    " non-cardiac, non-Chinese studies (cumulative N = ",
    format(bsa_sens_final$n_cumulative, big.mark = ","),
    "), the pooled OR is **", sprintf("%.2f", bsa_sens_final$or_median),
    "** (95% CrI: ", sprintf("%.2f", bsa_sens_final$or_lower), " to ",
    sprintf("%.2f", bsa_sens_final$or_upper), "), with Pr(OR < 1) = ",
    sprintf("%.1f%%", 100 * bsa_sens_final$pr_benefit), ".\n\n", sep = "")

if (bsa_sens_crossed) {
  cat("The efficacy boundary has been crossed even in this more homogeneous subgroup.\n\n")
} else {
  cat("The 95% CrI clearly includes 1.0 (upper bound ", sprintf("%.2f", bsa_sens_final$or_upper),
      "), indicating **no evidence of benefit** in this more generalisable population. ",
      "The apparent treatment effect observed in the full cohort is driven entirely by studies conducted ",
      "in cardiac surgery and/or Chinese populations.\n\n", sep = "")
}

cat("### What Does the Sample Size Determination Tell Us?\n\n")

cat("The sample size determination analysis asks: *'If we ran one more trial and added it to the existing evidence, ",
    "how likely is it that the updated meta-analysis would conclusively show benefit?'*\n\n")

cat("**For the full cohort** (all ", nrow(eeg), " studies):\n\n", sep = "")
cat("- Even a new trial of **", format(best_n_all, big.mark = ","),
    " patients** achieves only **", sprintf("%.0f%%", 100 * max_assurance_all),
    "** assurance of reaching a conclusive result\n", sep = "")
if (max_assurance_all < 0.80) {
  cat("- This is below the conventional **80% threshold** — meaning even the largest feasible trial size tested is more likely to *fail* to produce a conclusive meta-analysis than to succeed\n")
  cat("- The reason: the current posterior already incorporates ", format(sum(eeg$N_Total), big.mark = ","),
      " patients across ", nrow(eeg), " RCTs. A single new trial, however large, must shift a well-established posterior — and with substantial between-study heterogeneity, a new trial's effect may differ from the pooled estimate, diluting rather than strengthening the conclusion\n\n", sep = "")
}

cat("**For the non-cardiac, non-Chinese subgroup** (", nrow(eeg_filtered), " studies):\n\n", sep = "")
if (max_assurance_filt == 0) {
  cat("- Assurance is **0% for every candidate trial size tested** (up to ",
      format(max(ssd_filtered$total_n), big.mark = ","), " patients per trial)\n", sep = "")
  cat("- This is the most striking finding of the sequential analysis: **no trial of any realistic size can produce a conclusive result** in this subgroup\n")
  cat("- This occurs because the posterior for the pooled OR in this subgroup is centred very close to 1.0 (OR = ",
      sprintf("%.2f", or_filtered), ") with wide uncertainty. Adding a new trial cannot overcome the fundamental problem that the existing evidence shows essentially no treatment effect in non-cardiac, non-Chinese populations\n\n", sep = "")
} else {
  cat("- Maximum assurance is only **", sprintf("%.0f%%", 100 * max_assurance_filt),
      "** even at the largest trial size tested\n", sep = "")
}

cat("### Implications for Future Research\n\n")

cat("These results have important implications for whether further trials of EEG-guided anaesthesia for preventing postoperative delirium are worthwhile:\n\n")

cat("1. **The overall evidence is suggestive but inconclusive**: after ", nrow(eeg), " RCTs and ",
    format(sum(eeg$N_Total), big.mark = ","),
    " patients, the Bayesian sequential analysis has not crossed the efficacy boundary. ",
    "The treatment effect — if real — is modest (OR ≈ ", sprintf("%.2f", or_median),
    ") and heterogeneous across populations.\n\n", sep = "")

cat("2. **A single new trial is unlikely to resolve the question**: the sample size determination analysis shows that even a trial of ",
    format(best_n_all, big.mark = ","), " patients achieves only ",
    sprintf("%.0f%%", 100 * max_assurance_all),
    "% assurance for the full cohort",
    if (max_assurance_all < 0.80) " — below the 80% threshold conventionally considered adequate" else "",
    ". This is not because the question is unanswerable in principle, but because the existing evidence base is large enough that one additional trial has limited capacity to shift the pooled posterior decisively.\n\n", sep = "")

if (max_assurance_filt == 0) {
  cat("3. **For general (non-cardiac, non-Chinese) populations, the question appears unanswerable**: ",
      "the 0% assurance across all candidate trial sizes means the true treatment effect in this population ",
      "is likely close to null. Even a perfectly executed mega-trial of ",
      format(max(ssd_filtered$total_n), big.mark = ","),
      " patients would simply confirm the absence of a meaningful effect rather than demonstrate one. ",
      "Investing resources in such a trial cannot be justified on the basis of the current evidence.\n\n", sep = "")
} else {
  cat("3. **For general (non-cardiac, non-Chinese) populations**: assurance is very low (",
      sprintf("%.0f%%", 100 * max_assurance_filt),
      "), suggesting the treatment effect in this population — if it exists — is too small to demonstrate conclusively.\n\n", sep = "")
}

cat("4. **If any benefit exists, it may be population-specific**: the contrast between the full cohort (where the signal is driven by cardiac surgery and Chinese populations) and the restricted subgroup (where the signal disappears) suggests that EEG-guided anaesthesia may benefit specific patient groups rather than surgical patients in general. Future research may be better directed toward identifying *which* patients benefit (e.g., through individual participant data meta-analysis or targeted trials in high-risk subgroups) rather than conducting another broad RCT.\n\n")
```

## Limitations

1. **Small number of studies**: $k$ = `r nrow(eeg)` RCTs limits precision of heterogeneity estimates and power of subgroup analyses
2. **Heterogeneity**: substantial variation in surgical populations, pEEG monitors (BIS, SedLine, Narcotrend, AEP), delirium assessment tools (CAM, CAM-ICU, DRS-R-98), and follow-up durations
3. **Open-label design**: performance bias is possible — anaesthetists must see monitor readings to act on them; outcome assessor blinding partially mitigates this
4. **Disproportionate influence**: Chinese cohorts and cardiac surgery studies drive the overall effect; excluding them attenuates the pooled OR from `r sprintf("%.2f", or_median)` to `r sprintf("%.2f", or_filtered)` (95% CrI: `r sprintf("%.2f", ci_filtered[1])` to `r sprintf("%.2f", ci_filtered[2])`)
5. **Delirium assessment heterogeneity**: different tools (CAM, CAM-ICU, DRS-R-98, ICDSC, DSM-5) have different sensitivities and specificities, which may contribute to between-study heterogeneity

## Conclusion

```{r interpretation-conclusion, echo=FALSE}
#| label: interpretation-conclusion
#| output: asis

max_assurance <- max(ssd_all$pr_decisive, na.rm = TRUE) * 100
max_assurance_f <- max(ssd_filtered$pr_decisive, na.rm = TRUE) * 100

cat("This Bayesian meta-analysis of ", nrow(eeg), " RCTs involving ",
    format(sum(eeg$N_Total), big.mark = ","),
    " patients estimates that EEG-guided anaesthesia is associated with a pooled OR of **",
    sprintf("%.2f", or_median), "** (95% CrI: ", sprintf("%.2f", or_lower), " to ",
    sprintf("%.2f", or_upper), ") for postoperative delirium compared to usual care.\n\n", sep = "")

cat("**The evidence favours EEG-guided anaesthesia** — there is a ", sprintf("%.1f%%", 100 * pr_benefit),
    " posterior probability that the intervention reduces delirium. However, the 95% CrI ",
    if (or_upper >= 1) paste0("includes 1.0 (upper bound ", sprintf("%.2f", or_upper), "), ") else "just excludes 1.0, ",
    "and the Bayesian sequential analysis has ", if (!bsa_crossed) "**not** crossed the efficacy boundary" else "reached the efficacy boundary",
    ". The evidence is therefore **suggestive but not conclusive**.\n\n", sep = "")

cat("**This finding is robust** across model specifications (common-effect, random-effects, bias-corrected, ",
    "selection-weight, PET-PEESE, and RoBMA), prior choices (weakly regularising, vague, and sceptical), ",
    "and publication bias adjustments — all models agree that the pooled OR lies below 1.0.\n\n")

cat("**However, the benefit appears to be population-specific.** When cardiac surgery and Chinese studies are excluded, ",
    "the pooled OR rises to ", sprintf("%.2f", or_filtered),
    " (95% CrI: ", sprintf("%.2f", ci_filtered[1]), " to ", sprintf("%.2f", ci_filtered[2]),
    "), with $\\Pr(\\text{OR} < 1)$ = ", sprintf("%.1f%%", 100 * pr_filtered),
    ". In this more generalisable population, there is no convincing evidence of benefit.\n\n", sep = "")

cat("**The case for further trials is weak.** The sample size determination analysis shows that:\n\n")
cat("- For the full cohort, even a new trial of ", format(best_n_all, big.mark = ","),
    " patients achieves only ", sprintf("%.0f%%", max_assurance), "% assurance of a conclusive result",
    if (max_assurance < 80) " — below the conventional 80% threshold" else "", "\n", sep = "")
cat("- For the non-cardiac, non-Chinese subgroup, assurance is **",
    sprintf("%.0f%%", max_assurance_f),
    "** for every trial size tested — meaning no feasible trial can resolve the question in this population\n\n", sep = "")

cat("This combination of findings — a modest and inconclusive overall effect, driven by specific subgroups, ",
    "with no realistic prospect of a decisive future trial in the general surgical population — suggests that ",
    "the question of whether EEG-guided anaesthesia prevents postoperative delirium **may not be answerable ",
    "with conventional parallel-group RCTs and aggregate data meta-analysis**. Future research efforts may be ",
    "better directed toward individual participant data meta-analysis to identify patient-level predictors of benefit, ",
    "or toward targeted trials in the populations where the signal is strongest (e.g., cardiac surgery, elderly Chinese patients).\n")
```
